methods.scarAnswer: Sometimes yes. Sometimes barely. For many people, the scar is cosmetically better than the mole.

prosCons.pro1: Cleaner look where makeup sits better
prosCons.pro2: Less irritation from shaving, masks, collars
prosCons.pro4: Peace of mind once properly assessed
prosCons.con1: Possible scarring, depending on depth and method
prosCons.con2: Healing period where spot looks 'worse' first
prosCons.con3: Some moles may require more than one session

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Most mole removal is done with numbing medication, so the procedure itself should not be painful. You may feel mild stinging or burning for a few days afterward.
faq.q2: How long does it take to heal?
faq.a2: Recovery is often minimal, with mild redness or scabbing for a few days to a week. Laser healing is commonly quoted around 7 to 14 days.
faq.q3: Will mole removal leave a scar?
faq.a3: Scar risk depends on mole depth and removal method. Some people find the scar looks better than the mole once it fades.
faq.a4: Moles do not typically grow back unless they were incompletely removed.
faq.q5: How much does it cost in Malaysia?
faq.a5: Cost varies widely based on size, depth, location, and method. The most accurate way is a consultation and quotation.
